steps.title: Identify the target meaning
steps.content: The passage states wooden objects have “high susceptibility to rot”—meaning they normally deteriorate quickly. The discovery is “truly surprising,” and the word “but” signals a contrast: normally, wooden objects don't survive this long. The blank needs a word meaning “rarely” or “infrequently.”

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”Rarely” expresses the target meaning directly: wooden objects infrequently survive long due to rot, which explains why this 2,000-year preservation is surprising. The others either contradict the passage's point about susceptibility (sturdily) or lack the needed “infrequently” sense (carelessly, simply). This matches choice C — C is correct.
